name: LaVague
pricing_notes: Open source; LaVague Cloud (enterprise hosted) available with paid plans

auth.notes: Self-hosted; LLM provider credentials configured separately

performance.notes: Web automation; each browser action takes 1-5s; task completion in seconds to minutes

agent_readiness.notes: Web AI framework for browser automation via natural language. Uses Playwright under the hood. World Model (understand page) + Action Engine (generate Selenium/Playwright code) architecture. Integrates with LangChain and LlamaIndex agents.

gotchas: Web automation is inherently fragile (pages change, captchas, login flows)
Slower and less reliable than coded Playwright scripts
best_when: Agents that need to navigate websites without official APIs
Automating legacy web apps without API access
Integrating web browsing into LangChain/LlamaIndex agent pipelines
Prototyping web automation before committing to hand-coded scripts
avoid_when: When official APIs exist (use those instead)
summary: LaVague is an open-source AI web automation framework that converts natural language instructions to browser actions using LLMs + Playwright. World Model → Action Engine architecture. Integrates with LangChain and LlamaIndex. Best for agents needing to navigate websites without official APIs. Inherent fragility of LLM-driven web automation means production use requires careful monitoring.
